changelog.summary: Full rebuild against the OFFICIAL Claude Certified Architect — Foundations Exam Guide, Certification Terms and Conditions, and Certification Exam Policy (all three user-supplied PDFs published by Anthropic, Exam Guide dated 'Version 0.2, Last Updated: June 30 2026'). This is the highest-authority source used in this project — it supersedes v1/v2's web-search-derived exam facts and the generic Anthropic-docs-derived domain content entirely. Changes: (1) Corrected domain order/names/weights — D1 Agentic (27%), D2 Tool Design & MCP Integration (18%, was previously labeled D4), D3 Claude Code Configuration & Workflows (20%, was previously D2), D4 Prompt Engineering & Structured Output (20%, was previously D3), D5 Context Management & Reliability (15%, renamed from 'Context Management'). (2) Corrected exam mechanics: response format is single-answer 4-choice multiple choice ONLY (no multi-select — removed all 'multi' type questions), 4-of-6 scenario-bank structure, $125 exam fee, scaled 100–1,000 score, 12-month certification validity, pass/fail-only result reporting. (3) Replaced all 30 lessons with content organized one-per-official-task-statement (1.1–1.7, 2.1–2.5, 3.1–3.6, 4.1–4.6, 5.1–5.6), reflecting Claude Agent SDK specifics (stop_reason, Task tool, AgentDefinition, fork_session) that the generic Claude Code/Managed Agents docs used in v1/v2 didn't cover. (4) Added 12 official sample questions from the Exam Guide, clearly attributed and kept distinct from originally-authored practice questions, plus 22 new originally-authored questions covering the remaining task statements — all single-answer. (5) Rebuilt the glossary around official terminology. (6) Retired v1/v2 content now explicitly marked out-of-scope by the official guide (prompt-caching internals, streaming API, token counting) or superseded by the more specific official task statements (the generic 'five workflow patterns' framing, the broad Claude Code CLI hooks-lifecycle survey). D1's core loop/orchestration concepts and D3's CLAUDE.md/hooks/skills corrections from v2 were largely reusable and folded into the new task-statement lessons where they matched.

Closed the D2 verification gap using a user-run crawl of code.claude.com/docs (168 pages). Corrected memory-hierarchy model, added Auto memory and Routines, corrected hook exit-code semantics, corrected commands-merged-into-Skills. Superseded by v3's official-source rebuild.
Initial authored knowledge base, generic Anthropic-docs-derived.

primarySources.title: Claude Certified Architect – Foundations: Exam Guide
primarySources.note: Version 0.2, Last Updated: June 30 2026. The authoritative source for domain weights, task statements, exam mechanics, official sample questions, in-scope/out-of-scope topics, and preparation exercises. Highest-priority source in this project.

primarySources.title: Anthropic Certification Exam Policy
primarySources.note: Last updated June 25, 2026. Confidentiality of Exam Content, misconduct rules (no AI assistance during the exam), ID verification, 14-day appeal window.

primarySources.title: Certification Terms and Conditions
primarySources.note: Governs Program participation; 12-month certification validity/renewal; IP ownership of Exam content; used to inform the app's disclaimer language.

sources.title: Claude Managed Agents overview
sources.note: Background reading; superseded as primary D1 source by the official Exam Guide's Agent SDK-specific task statements.

sources.title: Multiagent orchestration
sources.note: Background reading; official Exam Guide's coordinator-subagent task statements (1.2, 1.3) are the authoritative D1 source now.

sources.title: Agent Skills overview / best practices
sources.note: Background; D3 Task 3.2 (official Exam Guide) is now the authoritative source for skills/commands content.

sources.title: code.claude.com/docs crawl (168 pages)
sources.note: User-run crawl (fetch_docs.py). Used for v2's D2 corrections; several facts (CLAUDE.md hierarchy, hooks, skills/commands merge, plan mode) carried forward into v3's D3 lessons where they matched the official task statements.

sources.title: Building effective agents (Anthropic Engineering)
sources.note: Background only in v3 — the official Exam Guide does not test the generic 'five workflow patterns' framing (routing, parallelization, evaluator-optimizer) as such; retired as a primary lesson, folded into background context on decomposition (D1 Task 1.6) only where it overlaps.
